message:
fix(text): improve width cache accuracy and wrap edge case handling

Fixes for text width calculation and line wrapping edge cases that
caused visual misalignment in certain Unicode scenarios.

width_cache.rs fixes:
- Correct width calculation for zero-width joiners (ZWJ)
- Handle variation selectors (VS15/VS16) for emoji presentation
- Fix cache invalidation for font metric changes
- Add bounds checking for grapheme cluster iteration
- Improve handling of ambiguous-width characters (East Asian Width)

wrap.rs improvements:
- Fix off-by-one in wrap point calculation at exact boundaries
- Handle word-break opportunities in CJK text correctly
- Improve hyphenation point detection for long words
- Add support for non-breaking space preservation
- Fix line count estimation for pre-wrapped content

Edge cases addressed:
- Emoji sequences with skin tone modifiers
- Combined diacritical marks (e.g., combining acute accent)
- Right-to-left text segments within LTR context
- Tab character expansion in mixed content
- Very long words exceeding available width

These fixes ensure consistent text rendering across different
terminal emulators and Unicode-heavy content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
